items.evidence_span: [6] R. de la Cruz and M. Araya-Polo, "Towards a multi-level cache performance model for 3d stencil computation,"
[7] R. de la Cruz and M. Araya-Polo, "Algorithm 942: Semistencil,"
[15] S. Lie, "Multi-million core, multi-wafer ai cluster,"
[25] R. Sai, J. Mellor-Crummey, X. Meng, K. Zhou, M. ArayaPolo, and J. Meng, "Accelerating high-order stencils on GPUs,"
[26] R. Sai, J. Mellor-Crummey, X. Meng, K. Zhou, M. ArayaPolo, and J. Meng, "Accelerating high-order stencils on GPUs,"
Most recently, Sai et al. [26] studied high-order stencils with a manually crafted collection of implementations of a 25 -point seismic modeling stencil in CUDA and HIP for the latest GPGPU hardware.
A further example is the semi-stencil algorithm proposed by De la Cruz et al. [7], which offers an improved memory access pattern and a higher level of data reuse.
